title: Past-only latent tyre-state feature via filtered degradation residuals
summary: Recent F1 work models lap time as fuel effect plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets.
rationale: Recent F1 work models lap time as fuel effect plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets. Your current features use observed degradation directly, but not a filtered hidden state that separates temporary noise from true stint decay.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race stint, estimate a simple one-dimensional filtered state from past laps only: residual lap pace after subtracting race-lap median, compound baseline, and lap progress trend; reset state after `PitStop=1`; expose filtered level, slope, acceleration, and uncertainty. Use a lightweight Kalman/state-space approximation rather than a full Bayesian fit.
expected_effect: Captures whether the car is genuinely ‘falling off the cliff’ versus just seeing one bad lap, improving stop timing precision.
risk: Bad normalization can confound traffic or weather with tyre state; full-sample smoothing would leak, so filtering must be causal.
